title: From-scratch rewrite of authentication guard and inventory add-item functionality

requirements.text: Completely rewrite the authentication guard in App.tsx from scratch. On every render of any protected route (Dashboard, Inventory, Billing, Reports), synchronously read the session flag from localStorage. If no valid session flag is found, immediately redirect to /login before mounting any protected page component. The login page must always be the first screen seen when the app is opened fresh or after logout. There must be zero path by which an unauthenticated user can reach a protected page.

quotes: the login page not showing on first load

acceptanceCriteria: Opening the app with no session in localStorage always lands on /login
Navigating directly to /dashboard, /inventory, /billing, or /reports without a session redirects to /login
After logging in, navigating to /login redirects to /dashboard
After logout, all protected routes redirect to /login
No flicker or momentary render of a protected page before redirect

requirements.text: Completely rewrite the Add Item form on the Inventory page from scratch. The form must collect Item Name (text, required), Category (select from constants list, required), Quantity (positive integer, required), and Price per Item (positive number, required). On submit, the form must call the backend addItem mutation with the correct argument shape, await the result, show a success notification, clear the form fields, and invalidate the inventory query so the table immediately reflects the new item. Eliminate all previous validation errors, mutation wiring issues, and query-invalidation gaps.

quotes: Inventory add-item functionality broken — items cannot be added from the Inventory page

acceptanceCriteria: Filling in all fields and clicking Add Item successfully creates the item on the backend
The new item appears in the inventory table immediately after submission without a manual page refresh
Submitting with any field empty shows an inline validation error and does not call the backend
A success toast or notification is displayed after a successful add
The form resets to empty state after a successful submission
If the backend returns an error, a clear error message is displayed to the user

constraints: All existing features (Dashboard, Billing, Reports, navigation, visual theme, print bill flow) must remain fully intact and unchanged
Hardcoded credentials must remain: username 'global Pride international school', password 'gpis@8320'
Session flag must be stored and read from localStorage
Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Backend Motoko actor must not have its data models or public API changed
nonGoals: Rewriting any other page or feature beyond the auth guard and inventory add-item form
Changing the visual theme or layout
Modifying the Billing, Reports, or Dashboard page logic
Adding new backend functions or changing existing backend function signatures
Implementing Internet Identity or any other authentication mechanism
